mechanism: Inhibits the BCL-2 protein, a key regulator of apoptosis, to promote cancer cell death.
additional_notes: Primarily used for chronic lymphocytic leukemia.

mechanism: Inhibits thioredoxin reductase, thus affecting cell survival pathways.
additional_notes: Traditionally used for rheumatoid arthritis.

mechanism: Induces apoptosis and promotes the degradation of the promyelocytic leukemia protein.
additional_notes: Effective against acute promyelocytic leukemia (APL).

mechanism: Modulates Hedgehog signaling pathway, impacting cell proliferation and survival.
additional_notes: Known to exhibit potent anticancer properties in various settings.

mechanism: Inhibits lysosomal enzyme activity, affecting autophagy and cell survival.
additional_notes: Commonly used as antimalarials and in autoimmune diseases.

mechanism: Inhibits cyclooxygenase-2 (COX-2) enzyme, reducing inflammatory processes.
additional_notes: Used in pain relief and cancer prevention studies.

mechanism: Inhibits aldehyde dehydrogenase, inducing oxidative stress in cancer cells.
additional_notes: Traditionally used to support the treatment of chronic alcoholism.

mechanism: Inhibits the Janus kinase (JAK) pathway, impacting cell proliferation and survival.
additional_notes: Predominantly used in myelofibrosis.

mechanism: Reduces hepatic glucose production; shown to affect cancer cell metabolism.
additional_notes: Primarily used for the treatment of type 2 diabetes.

mechanism: Inhibits the HMG-CoA reductase enzyme, impacting cholesterol synthesis and cell survival.
additional_notes: Widely used as a cholesterol-lowering drug.

mechanism: Anti-angiogenic and immune-modifying effects, impact on cancer cell survival.
additional_notes: Notorious for birth defects, but used in multiple myeloma under strict control.

mechanism: Inhibits protein synthesis in bacteria; has anti-inflammatory and apoptosis-inhibiting properties.
additional_notes: Used off-label for various inflammatory conditions.

mechanism: Intercalates into DNA and inhibits mycobacterial growth; may have anticancer properties.
additional_notes: Shows activity against leprosy and certain chronic inflammatory conditions.

mechanism: Antagonizes histamine H1 receptors, impacting allergic responses; shows cytotoxicity in cancers.
additional_notes: Mainly used for allergic conditions and as a sedative.

mechanism: Induces differentiation and apoptosis in cancer cells through RAR activation.
additional_notes: Extensively studied in clinical trials for various cancers.

mechanism: Interferes with DNA replication and transcription mechanisms.
additional_notes: Used in malaria and certain skin conditions.

mechanism: Disrupts microtubule function and cell division.
additional_notes: Commonly used for parasitic worm infestations.

mechanism: Modulates glutamate release, benefiting conditions like ALS and offering potential anticancer effects.
additional_notes: Approved for Amyotrophic Lateral Sclerosis (ALS).

mechanism: Chelates iron, interrupting fungal cell processes; shows potential anticancer effects.
additional_notes: Traditionally used in fungal infections.

mechanism: Blocks beta-adrenergic receptors, impacting heart rate, and exhibits anti-proliferative effects.
additional_notes: Used in cardiovascular disorders, with promising data in oncology.
